title: Context Selection, Compression, Deduplication, and Position · Language Clinic: Rag Context Compression Evidence
summary: A service stuffs every retrieved chunk into a long prompt, repeats overlapping text, truncates citations, and assumes a larger context window guarantees every passage is used. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the context compression evidence increment in citation-mapped context assembler. Learners progress th…
objectives: Compare extractive selection, sentence filtering, summaries, and structured compression against retained claim and citation evidence.
Collapse duplicate and overlapping chunks while retaining source diversity, conflicting versions, amendments, and dissent evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-context-compression-evidence — Compare extractive selection, sentence filtering, summaries, and structured compression against retained claim and citation evidence.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-context-selection-3
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-context-selection-3.
# Case 5798: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def worked_rag_context_selection_3(candidate_tokens: int = 6400, context_budget: int = 2200, selected_tokens: int = 1800, duplicate_chunks: int = 1, citation_links: int = 6, order_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "query3dekrow",
        "tenant5798",
        "indexworked35798",
        "prompt3dekrow5798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if min(candidate_tokens, context_budget, selected_tokens) < 0:
        return False, selected_tokens, "token-accounting"
    if selected_tokens > context_budget or context_budget > candidate_tokens:
        return False, selected_tokens, "context-budget"
    if duplicate_chunks < 0 or citation_links < 1:
        return False, citation_links, "context-identity"
    if not order_tested:
        return False, citation_links, "position-bias"
    retained = selected_tokens - duplicate_chunks * 100
    assert retained >= 0
    return True, retained, "citation-mapped-context"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare extractive selection, sentence filtering, summaries, and structured compression against retained claim and citation evidence.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Context Compression Evidence model · Theory Rag Context Compression E…, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Context Compression Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Context Compression Evidence model · Theory Rag Context Compression E…, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 1251 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to deduplicate, compress, order, and citation-map context within a measured token budget; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
